3.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Phage-DOMINANT (~80-95%+ of identifiable reads); eukaryotic papilloma/polyoma a small minority; large phage dark matter.
3.plausibility_verdict: NOT PLAUSIBLE / likely error. Observed phage 0%, euk 49%, unknown-fam 51%. Skin sebaceous virome is bacteriophage-DOMINATED (Cutibacterium/Staphylococcus/Corynebacterium phages). 0% phage contradicts primary literature; phage genomes appear missing or unlabeled.

citations.title: The human skin double-stranded DNA virome: topographical and temporal diversity, genetic enrichment, and dynamic associations with the host microbiome.
citations.supports: Healthy skin dsDNA VLP virome dominated by temperate tailed bacteriophages.

citations.title: The persistence and stabilization of auxiliary genes in the human skin virome.
citations.supports: Cutaneous bacteriophage populations across 60 subjects mainly tailed Caudovirales phages.

16.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Phage-DOMINANT; eukaryotic viruses only ~4% of reads (Jakobsen 2020).
16.plausibility_verdict: PARTIAL match with family-rank/proxy problem. Phage-present DNA-only structure is directionally correct, but eukaryotic fraction (25%) far above ~4%; top family Herelleviridae is a proxy artifact (large Firmicutes phages, not temperate Lactobacillus siphophages). Consistent with documented dairy-proxy limitation.

citations.title: Characterization of the Vaginal DNA Virome in Health and Dysbiosis.
citations.supports: Majority of vaginal viruses are dsDNA bacteriophages; eukaryotic viruses only ~4% of reads.

citations.title: Cervicovaginal DNA Virome Alterations Are Associated with Genital Inflammation and Microbiota Composition.
citations.supports: Lactobacillus bacteriophages closely associated with Lactobacillus abundance.

20.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Phage present and usually the largest identifiable fraction by richness, with a substantial signature eukaryotic DNA-virus signal (HPV, BK/JC).
20.plausibility_verdict: PARTIAL match. Top family Papillomaviridae well supported (HPV ~95% prevalence); DNA-only correct; Polyomaviridae expected. But phage at 14% with euk 68% likely under-represents bacteriophages.

citations.title: The human urine virome in association with urinary tract infections.
citations.supports: Most identifiable urine viruses were bacteriophages; HPV in 95% of subjects.

citations.title: Detecting viral genomes in the female urinary microbiome.
citations.supports: Female bladder virome contains bacteriophages and the eukaryotic JC polyomavirus.

citations.title: Longitudinal alterations in the urinary virome of kidney transplant recipients are influenced by BK viremia and patient sex.
citations.supports: Urinary virome carries anelloviruses and BK polyomavirus.

18.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Eukaryote-DOMINANT (paucibacterial surface; TTV/Anelloviridae + polyomavirus); low phage.
18.plausibility_verdict: GOOD match. euk 80%, phage 10%, top Anelloviridae, DNA 90%. Aligns with paucibacterial DNA-based ocular virome led by TTV.

citations.title: Paucibacterial Microbiome and Resident DNA Virome of the Healthy Conjunctiva.
citations.supports: Healthy conjunctiva paucibacterial; torque teno virus (Anelloviridae) frequent constituent.

citations.title: Elevated levels of Merkel cell polyoma virus in the anophthalmic conjunctiva.
citations.supports: TTV and Merkel cell polyomavirus frequently detected in healthy conjunctiva.

17.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Eukaryote-DOMINANT and Anellovirus-dominant (~97% of viral reads, Cebria-Mendoza 2021); herpesviruses minor; phage ~0.
17.plausibility_verdict: PARTIAL match with clear TOP-FAMILY RANK ERROR. Eukaryote-dominant DNA-based phage-~0 structure correct, but top family Orthoherpesviridae is WRONG: healthy plasma should be Anelloviridae-DOMINATED, herpesviruses secondary.

citations.title: Deep viral blood metagenomics reveals extensive anellovirus diversity in healthy humans.
citations.supports: Anellovirus sequences ~97% of total viral reads in 120 healthy donors.

citations.title: Human Anelloviruses: Influence of Demographic Factors, Recombination, and Worldwide Diversity.
citations.supports: Anelloviruses the major and most diverse component of the healthy human virome; detected in 86% of blood donors.

citations.title: Temporal dynamics of the lung and plasma viromes in lung transplant recipients.
citations.supports: In plasma, Anelloviridae most abundant, followed by Herpesviridae and Coronaviridae.
